codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit fixes `/exchanges/{id}` aggregate pair breadth, but it does not implement or verify any change for the assigned invalid-params timeout feature. The worker session only re-ran validators on an already-green branch and handed off success without evidence of a measured timeout remediation scoped to `tests/invalid-params.test.ts`.

issues.description: The assigned feature was to harden the representative snake_case invalid-params timeout path, but commit `ab66a27` makes no change to `tests/invalid-params.test.ts` or any app/setup code exercised by that test. The only shipped diff touches exchange-detail pair breadth, so the feature is not actually implemented in the reviewed commit.

This feature handoff points at commit `ab66a27`, whose code changes are unrelated to invalid-params timeout hardening: it swaps `/exchanges/{id}` aggregate `coins`/`pairs` to use raw ticker rows. Because the reviewed feature’s expected behavior concerns the runtime cost or timeout budget of the snake_case representative-response scan, this commit-to-feature mismatch means the reported success is unsupported by the actual shipped code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `compatibility-hardening-worker` as followed, but the transcript shows no failing-test-first work, no diff, and no manual validation tied to the invalid-params timeout path. The skill currently assumes implementation work will occur, but this feature can also fail via bookkeeping/handoff mismatch where a worker validates an unrelated HEAD commit and reports the feature complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript session `46f683f5-39ae-4d91-968b-05cb66023575` contains only reads plus validator reruns, then `EndFeatureRun` reports success with commit `ab66a27ce63c7bb9cc8fc4172472212b35f39396`; `git show ab66a27` changes only `src/modules/exchanges.ts` and `tests/exchange-surface-parity.test.ts`.

summary: Fail: the reviewed handoff claims `baseline-invalid-params-timeout-hardening` succeeded, but the referenced commit is an exchange-detail semantics fix and contains no invalid-params timeout hardening changes or feature-specific implementation evidence.
